pattern: Interleaving values from both ends produces the k largest distinct gaps up front, after which appending the remaining numbers in order contributes only gaps of one, hitting exactly k distinct differences.
hints: Consecutive numbers differ by one, so a plain ascending list yields just a single distinct gap.
Zig-zagging between a low and a high pointer creates differences that shrink by one each step, generating many distinct gaps quickly.
Emit 1, k+1, 2, k, 3, k-1, ... for the first k+1 numbers to create k distinct differences, then append k+2, k+3, ..., n in order, all adding gaps of one.

edge_inputs.description: maximum k equals n minus one
minimum k one with larger n

stress.description: random n with a valid k strictly between one and n
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randrange(2, 200)
    k = rng.randrange(1, n)
    return [n, k]
